description: Medium-sized butterfly with iridescent wings that shimmer in the sunlight. Wing coloration varies from sky blue to deep violet. Antennae have fine, hair-like extensions. Prefers open fields and gardens, often seen near flowering lavender bushes.
name: Glitterus aurorae

description: Small, oval-shaped bug with translucent wings and a greenish body that emits a faint glow at night. Antennae resemble delicate filaments. Typically found on the underside of leaves in dense forests.
name: Luminastrum nocturnalis

description: Dragonfly species with metallic, multicolored wings resembling stained glass. The body is slender, and eyes have a shimmering, rainbow-like hue. Commonly seen near ponds and slow-moving streams.
name: Prismaticus geminus

description: Medium-sized cicada with transparent wings that have intricate, cryptic patterns resembling tree bark. Body coloration varies from brown to gray. Echolocation calls are rare, making them challenging to locate in deciduous forests.
name: Cryptocicada mysticus

description: Firefly with a bioluminescent abdomen that emits a steady, warm orange glow. Wing covers are brown with intricate patterns, resembling autumn leaves. Often found near rivers and marshes in late summer.
name: Polypoda fireflyensis

description: Small moth with wings resembling a holographic display, featuring shifting colors and patterns. Body is slender, and antennae have fine, feathery filaments. Frequents gardens and urban areas.
name: Iridesca chrysaloides

description: Beetle with deep red elytra and black spots. The body is oval-shaped, and antennae are short and serrated. Active during twilight hours, often seen on tree trunks in temperate forests.
name: Phosphora rubellus

description: Graceful wasp with translucent wings and an opalescent, pearl-white body. Delicate, thread-like antennae and a slender, elongated abdomen. Builds intricate nests in garden shrubs.
name: Euphonyra serenitatis

description: Large dragonfly with wings displaying a spectrum of vibrant colors. Body is stout, and eyes have a mosaic-like pattern. Frequents fast-flowing rivers and streams.
name: Lumidraconia polychroma

description: Leaf-mimicking bug with a flat, broad body resembling a dead leaf, complete with realistic veining and mottled colors. Antennae are short and inconspicuous. Found in deciduous forests and thickets.
name: Camouflagephila terrarium

description: Butterfly with wings adorned in neon-bright, radiant patterns. Body is robust, and antennae feature small, glowing tips. Prefers sunny meadows and fields.
name: Neonymphalis radiatus

description: Ground beetle with a reddish-brown exoskeleton and prominent, sharp mandibles. Elytra have fine, grooved lines. Often discovered beneath fallen leaves in temperate woodlands.
name: Prunuscarabus rufescens

description: Small, jewel-like butterfly with wings that sparkle like diamonds in sunlight. The body is slender, and antennae are short and clubbed. Spotted in flowery gardens and coastal habitats.
name: Diamantis glittoris

description: Beetle with bioluminescent spots on its segmented body, emitting a soft green glow in the dark. Elytra are dark brown with subtle, luminescent patterns. Found in caves and underground chambers.
name: Biolumis nocturnalis

description: Hemipteran bug with wings resembling ancient parchment scrolls, complete with cryptic inscriptions. The body is elongated, and antennae are long and slender. Inhabits old libraries and historic buildings.
name: Cryptoclytra mirabilis

description: Leaf-mimicking bug with broad, flat wings resembling a vibrant, green leaf with veins and serrated edges. Antennae are short and inconspicuous. Found in lush rainforests.
name: Phyllodes mimica

description: Beetle with iridescent, metallic-blue elytra and a shiny, emerald-green body. Antennae are long and segmented. Spotted in tropical jungles, often on colorful flowers.
name: Iridescorhina elytrosa

description: Moth with wings displaying intricate, ancient hieroglyphic-like patterns in various earthy tones. Body is stout, and antennae are feathery. Occurs in archeological sites and ruins.
name: Polychroma archaica

description: Arctic dragonfly with transparent wings and a frosty, ice-blue body. Eyes have a frosty hue. Thrives in icy tundra regions near glacial streams.
name: Glacialis borealis

description: Beetle with transparent wings and a spectral, ghostly appearance. Body is slender and colorless, except for faint, shifting colors on its back. Frequently found in haunted forests and eerie swamps.
name: Psilodermis spectrus

description: Delicate wings with a wingspan of 3 inches. Predominantly white with tiny blue specks spread throughout. Long, feather-like antennae. Larvae feed on pine needles. Active during twilight hours.
name: Anthroptila punctatus

description: Bright blue body, wings edged with a golden hue. Often seen hovering above ponds and lakes during springtime. Characteristic double-wing beat.
name: Hexaplectra azurea

description: Small, pale white body. Thin waisted with translucent wings. Frequents gardens, especially drawn to roses. Packs a mild sting.
name: Zephyrella albis

description: Bioluminescent spots on a dark carapace. Active at night, using its light to attract prey. Found primarily in humid regions.
name: Crysochila luminis

description: Perfectly mimics small, dry twigs. Brown and rigid, will often remain motionless when approached. Found in deciduous forests.
name: Phantasma mirus

description: Long, grasshopper-like body, but with feathery appendages that resemble bird plumes. Green with white markings. Can produce a melodic chirping sound.
name: Plumafera aerius

description: Moth with a crescent-shaped white mark on each wing against a starry black background. Only seen during full moons.
name: Stelladorum lunatus

description: Ant species that constructs intricate, multi-layered mud nests. Dark brown, with a slightly metallic sheen. Workers are aggressive when nest is threatened.
name: Terraformica argilla

description: Iridescent blue beetle often found near water sources. Appears to 'dance' on water surfaces using hydrophobic leg hairs.
name: Aquarapis gemma

description: Spider with a crown-like structure on its abdomen. Silver and gold patterns. Weaves webs with a unique hexagonal pattern.
name: Coronarachne diadem

description: Ruby-red dragonfly with black-tipped wings. Fast flyer, and can often be seen darting between flowers on sunny days.
name: Rubroptera rosetta

description: Butterfly with translucent wings showcasing veins filled with nectar-like fluid. Found in meadows, especially around daisies.
name: Nectarimbellis florum
